problem_decomposition: Ephemeral skill artifacts (~68 files) scattered in .claude/ root alongside permanent template infrastructure. Causes: cluttered directory, 15+ individual .gitignore entries with 57 artifacts missing, mixed ephemeral/permanent files.
constraint_enumeration: Hard: all path references must be internally consistent (hooks, registry, patterns, agents must agree); dynamic path construction in 4 locations; glob-based discovery in 3 locations; cross-skill artifact dependencies (deploy-manifest read by teardown/rollback/iterate); persistent vs ephemeral distinction (deploy-manifest, verify-history). Soft: minimize PR count, keep changes reviewable.
solution_design: 2-PR approach: PR1 creates directory + updates gitignore (safe, backward-compatible); PR2 atomically migrates ALL path references. Flat structure under .claude/runs/. Allowlist-based replacement (never blanket sed). Manual handling for dynamic paths, globs, makedirs calls.
self_check: Revised from 3-PR to 2-PR after critic identified TYPE B constraint: system silently broken between PRs. Revised artifact handling: deploy-manifest and verify-history flagged as TYPE C for user decision. Added comprehensive artifact list from all sources (not just state-registry).
output: Recommended: 2 PRs. PR1 (prep, ~3 files), PR2 (atomic migration, ~170 files). PR2 uses targeted allowlist sed + manual edits for 7 edge cases. Directory: .claude/runs/ with flat structure.
